methods.description: A transient simulation spanning 8000 years starting at the mid-Holocene to study natural vegetation and fire occurrence, excluding anthropogenic influences.

steps.description: Perform a transient simulation starting at 8000 cal yr BP with initial conditions from a control simulation with CLIMBA.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize and run transient simulation with CLIMBA starting at 8000 cal yr BP

steps.description: Include orbital forcing, fixed greenhouse gases, and aerosol concentration, ignoring changes in sea level and land ice.
steps.searchable_summary: apply orbital forcing and fixed greenhouse gases to simulation

steps.description: Restrict reconstructions and model data to between 8000 and 200 cal yr BP to exclude industrialization impacts.
steps.searchable_summary: filter simulation data to exclude post-industrialization period

methods.method_name: Model Output Processing for Charcoal Data Comparison
methods.description: Processing model output to compare with palaeofire reconstructions using two approaches: absolute values and Z-score transformations.

steps.description: Use grid box values to calculate regional numbers by weighting with area and summing up.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate regional fire activity by area-weighted summation

steps.description: Derive Z-score transformed time series and average them to achieve regional time series.
steps.searchable_summary: transform and average time series to Z-scores for regional analysis

steps.description: Apply a 250-year running mean filter to reduce high year-to-year variability.
steps.searchable_summary: apply running mean filter to smooth time series data

steps.description: Use Box-Cox transformation and normalization for reconstructions and model data over the full period.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Box-Cox transformation and normalization to data

methods.description: Configuration and execution of the CLIMBA model to simulate global carbon cycle dynamics and fire occurrence.

steps.description: Run JS-BACH offline for one model-year with climate and orbital forcing.
steps.searchable_summary: execute JS-BACH model with climate and orbital forcing

steps.description: Simulate potential burned area as a function of fuel moisture and wind speed.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate burned area based on fuel moisture and wind speed

steps.description: Calculate carbon emissions as a function of area burned and available fuel load.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate carbon emissions from burned area and fuel load
